answer:
A1. Definition -
ANN: Computational models inspired by human brain
BNN: Real biological nervous system (human/animal brain)
Structure -
ANN: Artificial neurons (nodes), layers (input, hidden, output)
BNN: Biological neurons with dendrites, axon, synapses
Signal Transmission -
ANN: Numerical values, weighted sums
BNN: Electrochemical signals
Learning Mechanism -
ANN: Algorithms (backpropagation, gradient descent)
BNN: Synaptic plasticity (learning via experience)
Speed -
ANN: Faster computation (electronic signals)
BNN: Slower (biological processes)
Energy Efficiency -
ANN: High computational power needed
BNN: Highly energy efficient
Fault Tolerance -
ANN: Less robust (depends on design)
BNN: Highly fault tolerant
A2. Backpropagation is a supervised learning algorithm.
Calculate error at output and propagate it backwards.
Relies on Chain Rule of differentiation. 
 Algorithmic Steps:
Initialize Parameters - Random weights and biases
Forward Propagation -
Input → Hidden layers → Output
Compute activation using activation functions
Compute Loss -
Compare predicted output with actual output
Use loss function (e.g., MSE, Cross-Entropy)
Backward Propagation -
Compute gradient of loss w.r.t weights
Apply chain rule
Update Weights - Use gradient descent:
Repeat - Iterate for multiple epochs until convergence
A3. Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is a deep learning model mainly used for image processing, which automatically extracts features using convolution filters
It works through layers like convolution, pooling, and fully connected layers to identify patterns and classify data.
Working of CNN - 
Input Layer - Image as matrix (pixels)
Convolution Layer
Apply filters/kernels
Feature extraction (edges, textures)
Activation Function - ReLU commonly used
Pooling Layer
Downsampling (Max pooling / Avg pooling)
Reduces dimensions
Fully Connected Layer - Flattening + classification
Output Layer - Softmax / Sigmoid
	Workflow: Image → Convolution → Activation → Pooling → Flatten → Dense → Output
A4. RNN is a neural network designed for sequential data processing. It uses recurrent connections to retain past information. Architectural Components -
Input Layer - Takes input sequence xt​ at each time step
Hidden Layer (Recurrent Layer) -
Stores information (memory)
Depends on current input and previous hidden state
Recurrent Connection -
Feedback loop from hidden state to itself
Enables sequence learning
Output Layer - Produces output yt
Workflow : input layer → Hidden Layer → Output Layer
A5. LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) is a special type of RNN designed to capture long-term dependencies.
It overcomes the vanishing gradient problem of traditional RNNs.
Components - 
Cell State (Memory) - Long-term memory
Gates in LSTM
Forget Gate - Decides what to discard
Input Gate - Decides what to store
Output Gate - Decides output
Gate activations using sigmoid/tanh
Working Flow Input → LSTM cell state → LSTM Layers → Fully connected output layer 
A6. A GAN consists of two models: a Generator that creates fake data and a Discriminator that tries to distinguish real from fake data.
Both models compete with each other, improving over time until the generated data becomes highly realistic.
Generator (G) - Generates fake data from noise
Discriminator (D) - Distinguishes real vs fake data
Adversarial Process - G tries to fool D and D tries to detect fake data
Training Process
Step 1: Train discriminator
Step 2: Train generator
Repeat iteratively
Workflow:
Random noise → Generator → Fake data
Real + Fake → Discriminator → Classification → Generator Loss → Discriminator Loss 
Feedback improves both networks
